title: Monsoon tracker highlights: IMD forecasts thunderstorm in 11 districts of Telangana
content: According to the IMD’s latest bulletin, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds are very likely to occur at isolated places in 11 districts of Telangana

title: Delhi Court Refuses Interim Relief to Indian Polo Association in Jaipur Polo Ground Dispute
content: "A similar kind of request was moved before the Learned Principal District and Sessions Judge, PHC and Delhi High Court, and no relief was granted to the appellant. Therefore, keeping in view judicial discipline and propriety, I am not inclined to stay the execution of the impugned order even till next date"

title: Police step up probe into alleged human trafficking case
content: The police suspect a larger human trafficking network after rescuing victims allegedly duped with promises of migration to Canada

title: Attacks on vessels with Indian seafarers, Iranian ‘service fee’ may feature in IORA meet
content: India is the current Chair of the Indian Ocean Rim Association; Although the IORA’s charter explicitly bars members from raising issues that do not concern regional cooperation, in the broader scheme of things, India could look to utilise the forum for airing its concerns in the maritime space

title: Watch: The ₹370 Biryani Row: What really happened
content: A stand-up comedy audience interaction has snowballed into one of India’s most talked-about controversies. Comedian Pranit More is facing legal scrutiny after viral clips from his Gurugram show triggered outrage online and prompted action from authorities.

title: Union Urban Affairs Ministry takes physical possession of Jaipur Polo Ground in the Race Course Area
content: On May 20, an eviction order was issued which sought possession of the land parcel, citing its need for a “larger public purpose”

title: U.P. govt. constitutes SIT to probe allegations of ‘missing donations’ at Ram Temple
content: The SIT has been formed following a request from the Shree Ram Janmabhoomi Temple Trust and it has been directed to submit a final report within 15 days and preliminary report within seven days

title: Some artisans hang on despite hardships
content: While procuring sandalwood for artisans is an expensive affair, shortage of sandal in the market has made them switch their jobs; most artisans across the State are the last in their family to be in the profession of woodcarving

title: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami chief seeks clarification on High Commissioner-designate Dinesh Trivedi’s remarks
content: Tarique Rahman government should seek clarification from Mr. Trivedi regarding what he meant by the phrase ‘India and Bangladesh becoming one’, says Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami’s (JeI) Ameer Shafiqur Rahman

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay ‘shops’ AIADMK MLAs, alleges Stalin
content: The DMK President said democratic forces should also question what the MLAs had received in return for joining the TVK

title: KSHDCL revives 50% sandalwood subsidy scheme for artisans after 6 years
content: Given the limited availability of raw material, only a section of artisans is likely to get the material in the first round.

title: CM instructs Deputy Commissioners and SPs to remain at headquarters, be present in offices at 10 a.m. to address public grievances
content: He instructs district-level officers to visit every taluk and convene meetings with block-level officials to ensure proper enumeration of voters during the ongoing Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls

title: Government school students account for 96.7% of RGUKT admissions
content: University releases provisional list of 4,040 candidates for admission to its six-year integrated B.Tech programme across four campuses

title: Kochi cyber police to submit report on complaint against YouTuber
content: Cyber police are preparing the report following the State Police Chief’s order for a probe into a series of allegations against the YouTuber and his former associates, all of whom are popular on streaming platforms

title: Attacks on ships ‘unacceptable’, says India; Trump blames Iran
content: Three ships attacked by U.S. had strong Indian links going beyond crew onboard; External Affairs Ministry summons Charge d’Affaires of U.S. Embassy Jason Meeks and lodges ‘strong protest’ for second time

title: Three close contacts of Nipah-infected person in Kozhikode test negative for virus
content: Revenue Minister A.P. Anil Kumar, who is in charge of Kozhikode district, says there is no large-scale spread of the infection at present and no need to declare a containment zone

title: Amit Shah directs MHA support to panel studying demographic changes, especially in border areas
content: The panel held its first meeting on June 2; Saturday’s meeting was attended by top MHA officials, but Census Commissioner was only panel member present; MHA to provide logistical and other support

title: ED questions Trinamool MLA Madan Mitra, searches his house in gold-for-jobs
content: Mitra allegedly gave jobs to undeserving candidates in various municipalities in exchange for gold and cash

title: Mysuru hosts festival celebrating indigenous seeds, conservators
content: The Traditional Seed Conservator Award–2026 was presented to six farmers for their contributions to preserving indigenous seeds, maintaining community seed banks, reviving rare crop varieties, and creating awareness among farming communities

title: Government hospitals record sharp rise in surgeries, diagnostic tests: Health Minister
content: Staff attendance rises to 83.75%, while surgeries and diagnostic tests increase by 37.38% and 56.14%, respectively, over the past two years, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: CBSE schools in Bengaluru go beyond textbooks to instil moral values, address social isolation
content: Some schools have also come out with a framework to address digital wellness and screen time for students

title: Sreedharan’s high-speed rail will not solve Kerala’s travel problems, says forum
content: The technocrat has proposed a gauge and speed similar to the K-Rail project and this will not address the requirements of the people, the K-Rail SilverLine Virudha Janakeeya Samithi has said

title: You can hug Modi, but not Pinarayi? CPI(M) general secretary M.A. Baby asks Rahul Gandhi
content: CPI(M) leader accuses Congress leader of aiding Central agencies; calls for political decorum within INDIA bloc

title: Upendra Kushwaha re-elected as RLM chief
content: Days after his son, Deepak Prakash, was denied an MLC nomination despite being a Bihar Minister, Rashtriya Lok Morcha chief says Prakash would remain in the Cabinet for as long as there’s an NDA government in the State

title: Tamil Nadu Woman Accuses Relatives Of Sexual Abuse Over Property Dispute
content: The survivor, an 18-year-old student, alleged that around 10 people attacked her and her family members during a confrontation in their village on June 3.

title: "Pakistan Will Disintegrate On Its Own": Himachal Governor On PoK Protest
content: Himachal Pradesh Governor Kavinder Gupta drew parallels between the current crackdown on protestors in PoJK and Pakistan's 1971 atrocities in East Pakistan that led to the creation of Bangladesh.

title: Centre Takes Possession Of Jaipur Polo Ground In Delhi
content: The Indian Polo Association termed the eviction "wrongful, arbitrary and contrary to law", and said it would pursue all available legal remedies to safeguard its rights and interests.

title: Gurugram Couple Claims IVF Clinic Swapped Samples, Twins Not Their Children
content: The test allegedly indicated that the embryo of another couple was implanted into Meenu Rathore's womb.

title: "Regret It, In Bad Condition": Man Behind "370 Biryani" Joke Tells Ex-Boss
content: In a video released by the Instagram account of Gurugram-based Starvik Design, the company from which Jangra was fired recently, he spoke at length with the company's founder, Vivek Vishwakarma.

title: Elon Musk, World's 1st Trillionaire, Lives In 400-Square-Foot House In Texas
content: While Elon Musk's fortune has reached unimaginable levels, his everyday home is a small prefabricated house in Texas.

title: 3-Member Special Team Formed To Probe Ram Mandir Donation Row
content: The SIT was constituted on the direction of Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath following a request from the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.

title: Violations Of Hormuz Blockade Won't Be Tolerated: Rubio To S Jaishankar
content: Rubio underscored that violations of the US blockade and the illicit transport of Iranian oil will not be tolerated, Pigott said in a statement here.

title: Wikipedia Can Be Biased On "Disputed" Issues, Says Co-Founder Larry Sanger
content: Responding to questions about whether Wikipedia can be trusted on issues concerning India and Hindus, Sanger said the encyclopedia generally performs well when presenting straightforward and undisputed facts.

title: Delhi Police Mandates Joint Foot Patrols On Weekends, 2 Weekdays
content: The move follows a recent meeting between the Lieutenant Governor, the police commissioner and other senior officials, during which emphasis was laid on proactive, visible and interactive policing as an effective deterrent against crime and traffic violations.

title: Cancer Patient, 3, Dies As Nurse Injects Him With Formalin At AIIMS Bhopal
content: The incident took place in December 2025. A criminal case has been filed against the nurses in connection with the fatal mistake.

title: Tamil Nadu Man Befriended Women, Made Them Share Private Videos, Arrested
content: Investigators said the accused, who runs a grocery shop in Salem, befriended women living in his neighbourhood and gradually won their confidence before engaging in private video conversations

title: Karnataka Farmer Hits Official With Slipper Over Delayed Compensation
content: The farmer was upset over what he claimed was unequal treatment and repeated delays in processing compensation-related matters.

title: AI, Space Exploration, Brain Implants: Inside Elon Musk's Business Empire
content: Elon Musk, the world's richest man and now first-ever trillionaire, controls a lot of different businesses. Here's a look at Musk's vast business empire.

title: Most Congress Breakaways Succeeded, BJP Rebels Failed: A History Of Revolts
content: The latest round of political speculation began with comments from Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut, who suggested that regional parties such as the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) and the Trinamool Congress should consider merging with the Congre

title: 11-Foot Shark Mauls Woman In Sydney, She Grabs Onto Paddleboat To Escape
content: The 35-year-old suffered serious leg and arm injuries in the attack at 11:15 am off Coogee Beach, a police statement said.

title: "Like Son": Kalyan Banerjee On Abhishek Banerjee After "Him Or Me" Ultimatum
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee's ultimatum directed at Mamata Banerjee came after her nephew Abhishek Banerjee changed his lawyer in a signature forgery case

title: Aim, Then Boom: Trump Shares Footage Of Strike On Venezuelan Cartel Leader
content: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said that the "infamous leader" of Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, also known as Nino Guerrero, was "successfully" executed.

title: Pak's Shehbaz Sharif Says US-Iran Deal Likely To Be Finalised In 24 Hours
content: Weeks of negotiations on a peace agreement after an April 8 truce have so far not yielded success, with US President Donald Trump repeatedly insisting a deal is near.

title: 'Body preserved with cold water': Indian sailor dies on ship near Oman; tensions delay aid
content: The Forward Seamen's Union of India is seeking assistance after a 35-year-old officer died on a vessel off Oman's coast. His body has been onboard for over two days without proper refrigeration, leading to a health risk for the crew. The union alleges delayed medical help due to regional tensions and is demanding body preservation, repatriation, and an investigation.

title: Explained: Why fuel, fertilisers & forex are so important right now
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman recently urged the country to focus on fuel, fertiliser and foreign exchange and underlined that PM Narendra Modi’s appeal to conserve foreign exchange was "very important" amid the Middle East conflict.

title: Folarin Balogun and the joy of the global football
content: Folarin Balogun, scorer of two goals on his World Cup debut, was born in Brooklyn to Nigerian parents after his pregnant mother, visiting her sister in America, was reportedly advised against flying back to Britain, where she lived at that time. In the more extreme corners of the MAGA universe, that would make Balogun an "anchor baby" – born to someone visiting the US. On Thursday night, he was simply called "Man of the Match" the undisputed hero of a squad that perfectly represents the beautiful, messy, borderless reality of modern soccer.

title: 'INDIA bloc has already won 2029 elections': Rahul Gandhi lays out strategy of 'resistance with unity'
content: Rahul Gandhi boldly proclaimed that the INDIA alliance is already the victor of the 2029 Lok Sabha elections. He stressed the vital importance of uniting the opposition to challenge the BJP's perceived encroachment on democratic institutions and the silencing of dissenting voices.

title: US ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 has an Amazon 'link'
content: The US government has ordered AI firm Anthropic to halt access to two of its models. This decision stems from national security concerns. Anthropic disagrees, citing a limited jailbreak technique. Amazon researchers reportedly demonstrated this technique. The company states the identified vulnerabilities were minor and already known. The shutdown affects users worldwide.

title: Rohit Sharma scripts history, becomes only second Indian opener to...
content: Rohit Sharma achieved a significant milestone, becoming the second Indian opener to score 16,000 international runs. This landmark was reached during the first ODI against Afghanistan. Sharma joins an elite group of opening batsmen in cricket history. He also surpassed Jacques Kallis' ODI run tally. The Indian captain continues to set new records.

title: Humiliation by colleagues, harassment by 'online friend': Pune TCS techie suicide case gets murkier
content: A 48-year-old TCS employee in Pune died by suicide, allegedly due to workplace harassment, difficulties with leave after a family bereavement, and stress from his wife's chronic illness. A suicide note named two female colleagues and a man, leading to an abetment to suicide case registered by police.

title: Bill Gates isn't happy with US govt taking stake in Intel, IBM & other US companies
content: Bill Gates has raised concerns over the Trump administration taking equity stakes in Intel, IBM and other US companies, saying that the "rules of the game are pretty unclear right now." The Microsoft co-founder warned Washington could favour firms it owns over rivals with better technology. His comments come as Trump plans to meet AI executives from OpenAI, Anthropic and others to discuss similar government stakes.

title: Josh Allen admits NFL work cannot match one brutal farm chore
content: Josh Allen’s farm-work quote resurfaced after Touchdown Wire revisited the Buffalo Bills quarterback’s California roots. Allen compared NFL training to moving irrigation pipe in 115-degree heat, making it clear that football is hard, but farm labor shaped his work ethic long before he became one of the league’s biggest stars and carried Buffalo’s offense.

title: UP horror: Brother kills married sister after she insists on living with boyfriend
content: A 22-year-old man allegedly strangled his 20-year-old sister to death and dumped her body in a well. The accused confessed to the crime, stating the murder occurred after an argument about his sister's insistence on living with her boyfriend despite being recently married. Police have recovered the body and are investigating further.

title: Watch: SriLankan Airlines flight with over 200 onboard hit by lightning; lands safely
content: A SriLankan Airlines flight bound for Sydney was forced to return to Colombo on Friday after being struck by lightning. Passengers reported hearing a loud bang and seeing sparks from an engine. The crew managed the situation, and the aircraft landed safely back in Colombo due to minor engine damage.

title: Rohit Sharma surpasses Mohinder Amarnath, becomes oldest male cricketer to ...
content: At 39 years and 75 days, he went past former India all-rounder Mohinder Amarnath, who was 39 years and 36 days old when he played his last ODI for India in 1989.

title: Darnell Nurse trade request leaves Edmonton Oilers facing difficult NHL offseason decisions ahead
content: The Edmonton Oilers face a difficult challenge heading into the NHL offseason, with Darnell Nurse reportedly looking for a fresh start. There's been some trade speculation, but the veteran defenseman's large contract could scare off suitors. It would take a lot of salary gymnastics to make any deal happen, which is why it is one of the most closely followed NHL updates.

title: Himanshu Jangra says he regrets going to Pranit More's show as 'Rs 370 Biryani' row continues
content: Weeks after a viral clip from a comedy show led to his job loss, Himanshu Jangra expressed deep regret for his remarks and attending the event. He admitted his on-stage story was improvised and not entirely true, explaining his comments were meant for entertainment, not reflecting his actual mindset.

title: Bullet train project: India installs tunnel hoods - how they keep your journey smooth
content: India's ambitious Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project is introducing tunnel hood technology for the first time, enhancing high-speed rail travel. This innovation manages air pressure changes in mountain tunnels, significantly reducing noise and ensuring smoother, quieter journeys for passengers and surrounding communities. The technology employs pressure-relief vents to gradually equalize air, mirroring global best practices for advanced rail infrastructure.

title: Forced to work 16 hrs daily, abused: West Bengal woman rescued from Gurugram flat
content: In a shocking incident, a woman from Birbhum, West Bengal, was freed from a high-security apartment in Gurugram after being reportedly trapped for more than two years under the horrific conditions of forced domestic servitude. Each day, she toiled for 16 hours amidst appalling abuse, her escape thwarted by stringent security measures.

title: Outstanding! Gill takes one-handed blinder in 1st ODI - Watch
content: Shubman Gill stole the spotlight with a spectacular one-handed diving catch as India tightened control in the rain-reduced first ODI against Afghanistan in Dharamshala. Afghanistan posted a strong start through Rahmanullah Gurbaz’s quick century, but India struck back with regular wickets. Debutant Harsh Dubey impressed with key breakthroughs, while Arshdeep Singh and Nitish Kumar Reddy supported well to restrict the visitors.

title: 48-ball mayhem! Gurbaz rewrites history with ton vs India
content: Rahmanullah Gurbaz smashed a record-breaking 48-ball century against India in the rain-affected first ODI. His explosive 102 off 51 balls, featuring eight fours and eight sixes, marked the fastest ODI hundred by an Afghanistan batter and the quickest in an India-Afghanistan encounter. Gurbaz's rapid fifty also secured a spot in Afghanistan's top ODI half-centuries.

title: INDIA bloc hits reset button: Why 5-point truce plan of opposition alliance may not work
content: The INDIA bloc attempted a reset with a five-point plan to counter the BJP, but internal state-level rivalries remain a significant hurdle. Despite national unity efforts, allies like Congress and Samajwadi Party, or Congress and AAP, clash in state elections, undermining the coalition's strength and relevance. Addressing these deep-seated conflicts is crucial for lasting opposition unity.

title: 'RSS most misunderstood': Mohan Bhagwat explains best way to understand Sangh
content: Addressing the RSS centenary celebrations, chief Mohan Bhagwat declared the organization the world's largest voluntary body, yet "most misunderstood." He explained the outreach aims to clarify misconceptions, noting external views range from paramilitary to gymnasium. Bhagwat emphasized direct engagement as the best way to understand the Sangh's true nature and its century-long mission of national welfare.

title: Sudarshan Chakra: India develops multi-layered air shield to stop missiles, drones & rockets
content: India is bolstering its air defence with the "Sudarshan Chakra" project, a multi-layered shield against evolving aerial threats. Recent successful trials of its Ballistic Missile Defence system and operational successes highlight India's commitment to intercepting missiles and drones, safeguarding national security against regional adversaries.

title: When 'Pirates of the Caribbean' actor Johnny Depp explained the beauty of emotions
content: Johnny Depp's Hollywood comeback is gaining momentum with upcoming films like 'Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol' and 'Day Drinker.' A resurfaced 1995 interview quote about love and anger, once a personal reflection, gained significant courtroom attention during his defamation trial. This highlights a complex personal narrative now intertwined with his professional resurgence.

title: 'Masked woman enters, exits building': CCTV clue deepens mystery in Delhi Govindpuri fire case
content: A CCTV clip shows a masked woman entering a residential building shortly before a deadly fire broke out, killing three family members. Investigators are analyzing her movements for any connection to the blaze, which initially suggested a short circuit. The footage captures her entering and exiting the premises before flames became visible.

title: Akbar, Genghis Khan and ironically Stalin: 8 people richer than Elon Musk
content: When we marvel at Elon Musk's astonishing trillion-dollar net worth, largely bolstered by SpaceX's remarkable achievements, it’s essential to draw parallels with history's most influential monarchs. Leaders like Mansa Musa, Genghis Khan, and Akbar the Great presided over vast empires whose economic legacies, when adjusted for inflation, dwarf Musk’s wealth.

title: Middle East peace 24 hrs away? Pak PM suggests US, Iran may finalise deal soon
content: Pakistan's PM Shehbaz Sharif hinted that a peace deal between the US and Iran could be finalized within 24 hours, marking a significant diplomatic breakthrough. Both nations have indicated progress, with an agreed-upon text reportedly reached. US officials, however, urge caution against speculation, emphasizing that economic benefits for Iran are contingent on fulfilling commitments.

title: 'You win a Test & can't celebrate?': Ex-England player blasts ECB amid Stokes row
content: Stokes came under scrutiny after footage emerged from post-Test celebrations at a nightclub following England's win over New Zealand at Lord's. Stokes and teammate Gus Atkinson were involved in an incident that also included Saracens academy rugby player Totoa Auvaa.

title: Found in Epstein files: Clue about missing German model surfaces after 11 years - report
content: A German model, Michele, missing for 11 years, has reappeared in the Jeffrey Epstein files. Model recruiter Daniel Siad allegedly discussed her with Epstein before her 2015 disappearance. Her family fears the worst. Authorities could not launch an active search without evidence of a crime. Her fate remains unknown.

title: NFL trade rumors: Broncos, Cowboys linked to Saints star Alvin Kamara
content: NFL trade rumors around Alvin Kamara are heating up as the New Orleans Saints evaluate their offensive future ahead of the 2026 season. The veteran running back remains under contract through 2026, but growing speculation links him with potential interest from the Denver Broncos and Dallas Cowboys. Both teams are seen as strong schematic fits due to Kamara’s dual-threat ability as a runner and receiver. With Sean Payton leading Denver and Dallas seeking backfield consistency, Kamara’s name continues to circulate in league discussions as a possible late-offseason trade candidate

title: Silent, cheap & deadly: Why lasers are a gamechanger in air defence - where India stands
content: India is rapidly advancing its laser weapon capabilities, joining global efforts to counter drones and missiles. Under Project Sudarshan Chakra, DRDO is developing high-energy lasers to bolster air defence, offering a cost-effective and sustainable solution. This strategic move aims to enhance national security and achieve technological self-reliance against growing regional threats.

title: India's Afghanistan Test hero Suthar shines on Warwickshire debut
content: Manav Suthar continued his impressive form by taking two wickets on his Warwickshire debut against Yorkshire in the County Championship, days after starring on his India Test debut against Afghanistan with a seven-wicket haul. Meanwhile, veteran pacer Jaydev Unadkat shone for Sussex, claiming 4/29 to help dismiss Glamorgan for 155.

title: Bullets rain at Chandigarh store: Cashier shot dead, 13 rounds fired; CCTV footage emerges
content: Unidentified assailants opened fire at a medical store in Chandigarh's Sector 11, critically injuring cashier Janki Das, who later died. Two masked men entered the store and fired multiple rounds before fleeing with a third accomplice waiting outside on a motorcycle. Police are investigating, collecting CCTV footage, and have set up roadblocks.

title: Harsh Dubey, Gurnoor Brar handed maiden ODI caps as India opt to field
content: India handed ODI caps to Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar for their clash against Afghanistan. Despite initial weather concerns, the match will proceed as a 25-over per side contest. Captain Shubman Gill won the toss and opted to bowl first, aiming to test new combinations ahead of the World Cup. Afghanistan, though preferring to bowl, expressed confidence in their batting.

title: Scientists have identified key ages when the body ages faster
content: New research reveals ageing is not a slow, predictable process.  Instead, the body experiences rapid biological shifts around ages 44 and 60.  These changes affect metabolism, organ function, and disease risk.  Understanding these turning points allows for proactive health measures.  Healthy habits adopted early can significantly impact well-being later in life.

title: 'Baseless': Iran lashes out after US blames Tehran of attacking ships with Indians onboard
content: Iran's embassy in India has dismissed US President Trump's accusation of a drone attack on Indian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as "baseless." The embassy stated that Washington is attempting to divert attention from recent US strikes on three Indian-crewed ships, which resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors.

title: Grand Congress reunification? Raut's big appeal to NCP (SP), other 'breakaway' leaders
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut urged former Congress allies to reunite, emphasizing the need to strengthen the party against the BJP. He dismissed claims of the Congress being a "sinking ship" and appealed to Sharad Pawar to lead the reunification efforts. Raut also criticized the BJP's historical contributions and accused Amit Shah of attempting to break opposition parties.

title: 'Male corpse private parts' joke row: MBBS student barred from entering college
content: KEM Hospital suspended MBBS student Sejal Pawar for 15 days following a controversial stand-up comedy video. An FIR has been filed against her and others for allegedly circulating objectionable content. The college cited her safety and well-being amid public backlash, recommending counseling and a detailed probe by a five-member committee.

title: 'Vijay on shopping spree of AIADMK MLAs': Stalin calls TVK govt BJP's 'xerox copy'
content: DMK president M.K. Stalin accused Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay's government of acting as a "xerox copy" of the BJP, alleging attempts to weaken the opposition by inducting AIADMK MLAs. Stalin criticized the government's approach to women's safety, claiming TVK functionaries were involved in recent crimes and that the party removed victims instead of taking action.

title: ‘Pruning of tree branches as monsoon preparatory exercise could have prevented the latest incident’
content: While the victims and act of nature are blamed respectively by the Discom and the municipal authorities, experts point to the absence of pre-monsoon maintenance as the sole reason behind the tragedies

title: Centre suspends Cell Broadcasting Services
content: The service is kept on hold until further instructions due to certain issues flagged by the competent authorities, says order issued by NDMA; according to sources, a disaster alert allegedly issued at midnight to the Prime Minister’s contact number forced the govt. to temporarily suspend the service

title: Heavy rains lash parts of Cyberabad; waterlogging and traffic snarls reported from several areas
content: According to data from Telangana Development Planning Society as of 6 p.m., Rajendranagar recorded a rainfall of 55 mm, followed by 26.3 mm in BHEL Factory, 19.8 mm in Lingampally, 13.3 mm in Gachibowli, 11.8 mm in Chanda Nagar, 10.8 mm in University of Hyderabad and 10 mm in Patancheru

title: Centre urges State to probe alleged irregularities in SCCL
content: Union Coal Minister Kishan Reddy addresses a letter to CM Revanth Reddy highlighting reports of missing coal stock, unethical practices in Singareni

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
